rationale: The cited paper appears only once in the body text, in the discussion, as a parenthetical support for the general idea that effects can be asymmetric. It is not used to build hypotheses, define key constructs, motivate the response surface method, or guide measurement/estimation choices in the citing paper. The citing paper's core argument and empirical analysis proceed independently of this reference, so its function is a passing contextual mention rather than a substantive dependency.
